about: Curie CLI: run `curie` for the interactive terminal, or pass a subcommand for scripts

args.help: Show verbose plumbing (helm/kubectl/rollout/port-forward)
Payload only; suppress progress and diagnostics
Colorize output
Machine-readable JSON to stdout; human/log text to stderr

name: curie

subcommands.about: Scaffold a new plugin bundle (Claude Code plugin shape)

args.help: Kebab-case plugin name (e.g. deal-desk). Omit when using --from-spec
Target directory; defaults to ./<name>
Scaffold non-interactively from an agent-authored spec file (JSON). The bundle name comes from the spec

Adopt an existing non-plugin directory: scaffold the plugin skeleton INTO it (alongside your code, never overwriting existing files), deriving the name from the directory unless a NAME is given. The on-ramp for a pre-plugin (agent-ss-template) bundle; port the logic by hand afterward (docs/adopting-a-bundle.md, #745)

subcommands.name: init

subcommands.about: Work with a local runner session for a plugin bundle: `skill <up|down|status|message|eval|approvals>`. `versions` and `memory` are answered here too, reporting that this tier has neither
subcommands.name: skill

subcommands.about: Boot a local runner container for the bundle and print the env summary

args.help: Plugin bundle directory
Runner image. Default: version-pinned `ghcr.io/curie-eng/curie-runner:<version>` on release builds; local `curie-runner` on dev builds. Pass to override
Host port for the local bot
Container name
Use the runner's scripted fake model (offline; no credential)
Docker network to join (e.g. curie_default for the dev stack)
OTLP endpoint for traces (e.g. http://otel-collector:4318)
ACI budget JSON for the session
Model id, forwarded as CURIE_MODEL. Omit for the SDK default. Setting it makes token usage attributable in Langfuse traces
Run the named model through local Ollama

Forward an environment variable BY NAME into the runner sandbox, so a bundle's authed MCP server can read a secret (e.g. an API token) the same way model credentials are forwarded: the value is read from your environment by docker and never placed in argv. Repeatable. Example: `--secret GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN` with that var exported

Opt-in: read a bundle-local `.env` (any dotenv path) as the LOWEST- priority model-credential source, so the bundle boots live with no `set -a; source .env` step. Precedence: shell env > stored secret (`curie secrets set`) > this file. Only CURIE_CREDENTIALS, CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY are read; every other key in the file is ignored (#749)
Remove a leftover container of the same name before booting, instead of failing on the conflict

subcommands.name: up

subcommands.about: Check that the bundle's MCP servers load in an offline runner container

args.help: Plugin bundle directory
Runner image. Defaults to the same image resolution as `skill up`
Check deadline in seconds, forwarded to the runner container

subcommands.name: check

subcommands.about: View the bundle's declared approval gates, or print the env assignment that sets or clears the runner's override (nothing is mutated)

args.help: Plugin bundle directory
Tool name to gate. Repeatable. Omit (with no --clear) to view the bundle's declared gates
Print the assignment that clears the env override

List pending approval RECORDS (not gate config). Accepted so it can be DECLINED with a reason at this tier rather than error like a typo: the skill tier's local runner keeps no durable approval store (ADR-0063, ADR-0077). Use `curie local/cluster approvals --list`
Resolve the approval with this id. Not available at the skill tier for the same reason as --list; declined with that reason
The actor resolving the approval (paired with --resolve on local/cluster). Accepted here only so a local/cluster-style resolve invocation is declined cleanly, not rejected as an unknown flag
Reject instead of approve (paired with --resolve). Accepted only to be declined cleanly at this tier

subcommands.name: approvals

subcommands.about: Not available at this tier: `skill up` runs the bundle bytes on disk, so no deployed version is assigned; use `curie local versions <agent>` or `curie cluster versions <agent>` for a deployed agent
subcommands.name: versions

subcommands.about: Not available at this tier: this tier configures no memory namespace: `skill up` never sets a memory ref, and there is no platform here to own or address one; use `curie local memory <agent>` or `curie cluster memory <agent>` for a deployed agent
subcommands.name: memory

subcommands.about: Stop and remove the local runner container

args.help: Container name to remove. Defaults to the recorded runner, then to `curie-runner-local`. Pass it to clear a leftover container from a directory with no `.curie/runner.json`

subcommands.name: down

subcommands.about: Show the local runner's session status

args.help: Runner base URL (defaults to the started runner, then localhost)

subcommands.name: status

subcommands.about: Send a synthetic event to the local runner and stream the reply

args.help: The message text
Synthetic Slack user id
ACI event type
Runner base URL (defaults to the started runner, then localhost)

subcommands.name: message

subcommands.about: Run the bundle's eval cases through the local runner

args.help: Eval case file (default: evals/cases.json here, then the running bundle's)
Runner base URL (defaults to the started runner, then localhost)

Run the suite against this model in a throwaway runner instead of the already-running one. Repeatable: pass it N times to sweep N models and report pass-rate per model (#526). Needs a model credential + Docker
Forward a connector secret BY NAME into each sweep runner (as `skill up --secret`), so an authed-MCP bundle can run under `--model`
Runner image for the sweep runners. Defaults to the same image resolution as `skill up`

subcommands.name: eval

subcommands.about: Interview to generate a starter `evals/cases.json` (guided eval generation)

args.help: Where to write the suite (default: evals/cases.json)
Overwrite an existing suite file instead of refusing

subcommands.name: eval-init

subcommands.about: Work with the local compose stack and local platform API
subcommands.name: local

subcommands.about: Bring the dev stack up (`core` with `--minimal`, else `full`) and print URLs. Add `--slack` for the optional dispatcher

args.help: Compose file. Default: version-pinned `compose.release.yaml` from the remote on release builds; local `compose.dev.yaml` on dev builds. Pass to override
Print the docker compose command and exit without executing
Bring up only the 7 core services (skip Langfuse/ClickHouse/OTel/UI)
Run the named model through local Ollama
Also start the optional Slack dispatcher (adds --profile slack)

Opt-in: read a bundle-local `.env` (any dotenv path) as the LOWEST- priority model-credential source, so the compose stack boots live with no `set -a; source .env` step. Precedence: shell env > this file. Only CURIE_CREDENTIALS, CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY are read; every other key in the file is ignored, and the value never reaches argv or logs (#749)

subcommands.long_about:
Bring the dev stack up (`core` with `--minimal`, else `full`) and print URLs. Add `--slack` for the optional dispatcher.

Model parity with `curie skill up`: `local up` runs the real model when a model credential is present in the shell, and the offline fake model otherwise. Providers are first-class beyond Anthropic: an Anthropic key (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` / `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`) OR the provider-agnostic `CURIE_CREDENTIALS` (with `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` for an OpenAI-compatible endpoint such as OpenRouter). Set `CURIE_FAKE_MODEL=1` to force the fake even with a credential; set `CURIE_FAKE_MODEL=0` (or provide a credential) to go live.
subcommands.name: up

subcommands.about: Rebuild + recreate ONE compose service (e.g. after a code change) without losing the stack's already-resolved credential/model-mode wiring

args.help: The compose service to rebuild, e.g. `curie-worker`
Compose file. Default: version-pinned `compose.release.yaml` from the remote on release builds; local `compose.dev.yaml` on dev builds. Pass to override
Print the docker compose command and exit without executing
Match how `local up` brought the stack up (core-only vs full)
Match how `local up` brought the stack up (--local-model, if used)
Match how `local up` brought the stack up (--slack, if used)

Match how `local up` brought the stack up (--env-file, if used): read a bundle-local `.env` as the LOWEST-priority model-credential source so the rebuilt service comes back on the SAME model as the rest of the stack, instead of reverting to compose's fake default (#853). Precedence (shell env > this file), the recognized keys, and the never-in-argv/logs masking are identical to `local up --env-file`

subcommands.long_about:
Rebuild + recreate ONE compose service (e.g. after a code change) without losing the stack's already-resolved credential/model-mode wiring.

A raw `docker compose up --no-deps <service>` silently reverts that one service to compose's fake-model/dev-stub defaults, because compose's `${VAR-default}` substitution reads THIS invocation's shell, not what the rest of the stack is running with -- export the same credential / CURIE_FAKE_MODEL you want, same as `local up`.
subcommands.name: rebuild

subcommands.about: Stop the dev stack (docker compose down), keeping volumes

args.help: Compose file. Default: version-pinned `compose.release.yaml` from the remote on release builds; local `compose.dev.yaml` on dev builds. Pass to override
Also destroy volumes (adds -v). Prompts for confirmation unless --yes
Skip the --wipe confirmation prompt
Print the docker compose command and exit without executing

subcommands.name: down

subcommands.about: Show the dev stack's service status (docker compose ps)

args.help: Compose file. Default: version-pinned `compose.release.yaml` from the remote on release builds; local `compose.dev.yaml` on dev builds. Pass to override
Print the docker compose command and exit without executing

subcommands.name: status

subcommands.about: Connect or disconnect the local compose stack from a real Slack workspace

args.help: Chat surface to configure. Required until the CLI grows more than one comms target
Clear Slack from the local stack instead of connecting it
The stack runs only the 7 core services (skip Langfuse/ClickHouse/OTel/UI). Must match how `local up` brought it up
Slack app token. Defaults from SLACK_APP_TOKEN
Slack bot token. Defaults from SLACK_BOT_TOKEN
Compose file. Default: version-pinned `compose.release.yaml` from the remote on release builds; local `compose.dev.yaml` on dev builds. Pass to override
Print the docker compose command(s) that would run and exit without executing

subcommands.name: comms

subcommands.about: Drive the local compose stack end to end with zero Slack contact

args.help: The user message text
Slack channel id to send as; must match the target agent's slack_channel. Omit to use the sole deployed agent's channel (errors if zero or multiple agents are deployed)
Existing thread ts to continue a conversation; omit to start a new thread. Pair with --channel to keep multi-turn context
Reuse the last turn's context (channel, thread, transport) recorded in .curie/last-turn.json in the working directory; type only the new message text
Valkey password (compose default `valkeypass`). Prefer the CURIE_VALKEY_PASSWORD env var over passing a real secret on the command line, where it leaks via `ps` and shell history
Local mode only: platform API base URL for the channel lookup
Platform API key for the default-channel lookup
Synthetic Slack user id for the enqueued event
Stream the dispatcher enqueues onto
How long to wait for the worker's reply before printing diagnostics. Default: 300 seconds
Print the queue and stub plan that a real run would produce, and exit

subcommands.name: message

subcommands.about: Run the bundle's `evals/cases.json` through the local tier and grade with the same grader `skill eval` uses (the per-tier parity gate)

args.help: Eval case file (default: `evals/cases.json` here, then the recorded bundle's)
Slack channel id to send as; must match the target agent's slack_channel. Omit to use the sole deployed agent's channel
Valkey password (compose default `valkeypass`). Prefer the CURIE_VALKEY_PASSWORD env var over passing a real secret on the command line, where it leaks via `ps` and shell history
Platform API base URL for the channel lookup
Platform API key for the default-channel lookup
Synthetic Slack user id for the enqueued events
Stream the dispatcher enqueues onto
How long to wait for each case's reply. Default: 300 seconds

Evaluate under this model instead of the deployed one; repeat to sweep several models in one run (#526). A sweep triggers a platform eval per model and reports the per-model pass-rate from `GET /evals/matrix`
Number of eval cases to run concurrently. Sequential (1) is the only supported value today; parallel dispatch is tracked in #709, so any value above 1 is refused rather than silently run sequentially
Print the plan that a real run would produce, and exit

subcommands.name: eval

subcommands.about: Push the bundle to the local platform API and deploy it

args.help: Plugin bundle directory
Platform API base URL
Platform API key
Slack channel to bind the agent to. On first create it defaults to C0LOCALDEV; on redeploy it is only moved when you pass this flag, so omitting it leaves the deployed agent's channel untouched
Target environment
Version label; defaults to <manifest version>-<unix time>

Bind a per-agent connector secret by NAME (ADR-0009, #429). The value is resolved from your environment or the host secret vault (`curie secrets set <NAME>`) and sent to the platform, which stores it on the agent so the worker forwards it into the sandbox for a bundle's authed MCP server. The value never appears in argv. Repeatable

subcommands.about: Force a stuck thread's sandbox to be released (`POST /agents/{id}/threads/{thread_key}/reset`, #737). The worker's next maintenance tick deletes the thread's claim and route, so its next message cold-creates a fresh sandbox instead of adopting one that may be running stale env. Interrupts a live turn on the thread first, so it requires --yes; does not delete conversation history

args.help: Agent name or id (scopes the action; the release is thread-keyed)
The thread key to reset (e.g. the Slack thread ts)
Confirm the action; it interrupts any live turn on the thread

subcommands.name: reset-thread

subcommands.about: Work with the deployed cluster release and platform API
subcommands.name: cluster

subcommands.about: Install or upgrade the Curie release via Helm (helm upgrade --install). By default it puts the UI and Langfuse on node ports for tailnet/LAN access; pass --no-expose to keep them ClusterIP-only. Set CURIE_CREDENTIALS (an Anthropic API key; CURIE_MODEL_CREDENTIALS is a deprecated alias) to install with the real model; without it the install is sealed (fake model, canned replies). A real model is still unreachable behind the fail-closed sandbox until you open its egress with --allow-egress-host <provider> (or --allow-web-egress <CIDR> for a raw range)

args.help: Kubernetes namespace
Helm release name
Helm chart. Default: the version-pinned chart release asset on release builds; local `charts/curie` on dev builds. Pass a path or ref to override
Keep the UI and Langfuse services ClusterIP instead of NodePort
Force the sealed fake-model install even when CURIE_CREDENTIALS is set (dev/CI escape hatch); suppresses the fake-model warning
Run the named model through the chart inference deployment
Open runner egress to a named model provider's API host(s), resolved to narrow host routes at install time (repeatable). One of: anthropic, openrouter. For a raw CIDR, use --allow-web-egress
Open runner egress to a declared destination for skill web access, repeatable CIDR, TCP 443. Additive to the provider egress; omit to stay fully sealed
Extra `--set KEY=VAL` passed through to helm verbatim (repeatable)
Install with the chart's built-in dev-default secrets instead of generating strong per-release randoms. Deterministic, for local dev and CI only -- these defaults are published in the public repo
Print the helm command that would run and exit without executing

subcommands.about: Uninstall the release and sweep its runtime namespaces (helm uninstall + kubectl delete namespace). The agents.x-k8s.io CRDs are left in place

Helm release name
Skip the interactive confirmation prompt
Print the commands that would run and exit without executing

args.help: The user message text
Slack channel id to send as; must match the target agent's slack_channel. Omit to use the sole deployed agent's channel (errors if zero or multiple agents are deployed)
Existing thread ts to continue a conversation; omit to start a new thread. Pair with --channel to keep multi-turn context
Reuse the last turn's context (channel, thread, transport) recorded in .curie/last-turn.json in the working directory; type only the new message text
Kubernetes namespace of the release. Default: curie
Helm release name. Default: curie
Helm chart. Default: the version-pinned chart release asset on release builds; local `charts/curie` on dev builds. Pass a path or ref to override
Host the in-cluster worker uses to reach the stub. Omit to auto-detect the local IP the kernel would use to reach the cluster
Port the stub binds (0.0.0.0); the worker posts here
Local port the Valkey port-forward binds

Valkey password. Omit to read the release's own password from its chart Secret. Prefer the CURIE_VALKEY_PASSWORD env var over passing a real secret on the command line, where it leaks via `ps` and shell history
Local port the API port-forward binds (default-channel lookup)
Platform API key for the default-channel lookup. Omit to read the release's own key from its chart Secret
Synthetic Slack user id for the enqueued event
Stream the dispatcher enqueues onto

How long to wait for the worker's reply before printing diagnostics. Defaults high because the worker kernel can retry a run up to 3 times with a 90s sandbox-claim timeout each (worst case near 270s of claim waits alone), so a shorter ceiling can time out while it is still working. Default: 300 seconds
Print the kubectl commands, stub URL, and enqueue description that a real run would produce, and exit without executing anything

Evaluate under this model instead of the deployed one; repeat to sweep several models in one run (#526). A sweep triggers a platform eval per model and reports the per-model pass-rate from `GET /evals/matrix`
Number of eval cases to run concurrently. Sequential (1) is the only supported value today; parallel dispatch is tracked in #709, so any value above 1 is refused rather than silently run sequentially

subcommands.name: eval

subcommands.about: Push the bundle to the platform API and deploy it

args.help: Plugin bundle directory
Platform API base URL. Omit to self-plumb a kubectl port-forward to the release's api service (a loopback tunnel); CURIE_API_URL or an explicit value direct-dials the given URL with no tunnel
Kubernetes namespace of the release (for the port-forward + key discovery). Default: curie
Helm release name (for the port-forward + key discovery). Default: curie

Platform API key. Omit to auto-discover the release Secret key (`<release>-secrets`); the discovered key travels only in the X-API-Key header over the loopback tunnel, never over the cleartext NodePort proxy (ADR-0057). An explicit value wins
Slack channel to bind the agent to. On first create it defaults to C0LOCALDEV; on redeploy it is only moved when you pass this flag, so omitting it leaves the deployed agent's channel untouched
Target environment
Version label; defaults to <manifest version>-<unix time>

Per-agent connector secrets are NOT yet delivered at the cluster tier (#440): this flag is accepted only so it can be DECLINED with a reason instead of erroring like a typo. Until per-agent K8s Secret + secretKeyRef delivery lands, a value-only SandboxClaim CR would persist the token in plaintext in etcd. Use `curie local deploy --secret` today. See ADR-0009

Local tier only; use `cluster deploy --plugin-dir agents/<folder>` for the cluster tier. (Bare `deploy` is a retired pre-tier-split verb name -- see `retired.rs` -- so this spells out the tier it targets.)

Version label; defaults to <manifest version>-<unix time>
Bind a per-agent connector secret by NAME (ADR-0009, #429). The value is resolved from your environment or the host secret vault (`curie secrets set <NAME>`) and sent to the platform. Repeatable

subcommands.name: deploy-local

subcommands.about: Build the runner image locally from `runner/Dockerfile` (source checkout only)

args.help: Image tag to build

subcommands.long_about:
Build the runner image locally from `runner/Dockerfile` (source checkout only).

Runs `docker build -f runner/Dockerfile -t <tag> .` from the repo root. A release binary pulls the pinned runner image from GHCR automatically and never needs this; it errors clearly if Docker is missing or there is no repo checkout.
subcommands.name: build

subcommands.about: Bootstrap or update a dev checkout: install deps and build, start nothing (source checkout only)

args.help: Reuse already-present artifacts while refreshing dependencies and builds

subcommands.long_about:
Bootstrap or update a dev checkout: install deps and build, start nothing (source checkout only).

From the repo root, runs (each idempotent, streaming output): copy `.env.example` to `.env` if missing, `uv sync`, `pnpm install` in `apps/ui`, `cargo install --path cli` (builds AND puts `curie` on PATH, so re-running install refreshes the live CLI), then builds the runner image. With `--update`, already-present heavyweight artifacts like the runner image are reused. `curie update` is the fast CLI-only subset. A release binary has no source tree to install and errors clearly; a missing tool (uv/pnpm/cargo/docker) prints a pointer and stops.
subcommands.name: install

subcommands.about: Rebuild this CLI from the source checkout and reinstall it on PATH (source checkout only)

args.help: Also rebuild the local runner image (for runner/ changes)

subcommands.long_about:
Rebuild this CLI from the source checkout and reinstall it on PATH (source checkout only).

The fast per-change refresh: runs `cargo install --path cli --force` from the repo root so a code change to the CLI is live on the next `curie` invocation, without re-running the bootstrap script. Pass `--image` to also rebuild the local runner image (for `runner/` changes). A release binary cannot rebuild itself and errors clearly.
subcommands.name: update

subcommands.about: Open the interactive terminal interface
subcommands.long_about:
Open the interactive terminal interface.

A keyboard-driven terminal UI for humans: browse targets and actions, preview exact commands, fill required values, and run workflows without memorizing the full command surface.
subcommands.name: interactive

subcommands.about: Store and manage local secrets in Curie private storage
subcommands.name: secrets

subcommands.about: Save a secret in Curie private storage. Prompts with hidden input by default

args.help: Environment-variable-style secret name, e.g. GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN
Read the value from another environment variable instead of prompting

subcommands.name: set

subcommands.about: List saved Curie secret names. Values are never printed
subcommands.name: list

subcommands.about: Remove a saved secret

args.help: Environment-variable-style secret name

subcommands.name: unset

subcommands.about: Run a repo dev script (contracts, chart-check, e2e) -- source checkout only
subcommands.long_about:
Run a repo dev script (contracts, chart-check, e2e) -- source checkout only.

Thin wrappers over the repo's dev scripts so contributors get a unified `curie <command>` surface; the scripts stay the implementation. A release binary has no scripts and errors clearly.
subcommands.name: dev

subcommands.about: Check the frozen contracts (`bash scripts/check-contracts.sh`)
subcommands.name: contracts

subcommands.about: Render-assert the Helm chart (`bash charts/curie/ci/render-assertions.sh`)
subcommands.name: chart-check

subcommands.about: Run the scripted CLI end-to-end test (`bash cli/scripts/e2e.sh`)
subcommands.name: e2e

subcommands.about: Run the cold-start parity ladder across the skill, local, and cluster tiers, fake model by default (#690, `bash cli/scripts/e2e-ladder.sh`)
subcommands.name: e2e-ladder

subcommands.about: Runtime E2E the Helm chart on a local cluster: install a trimmed slice, seed a bundle into MinIO, run the sandbox bundle-fetch init pair, and exec-assert the runner's view -- the one-command way to satisfy a chart/sandbox runtime acceptance criterion static checks cannot (#199, `bash scripts/chart-runtime-e2e.sh`)
subcommands.name: chart-runtime-e2e

subcommands.about: Lint the interface catalog docs (`bash scripts/check-docs.sh`)
subcommands.name: docs-lint

subcommands.about: Validate every `examples/` bundle against Claude Code (`bash scripts/check-plugin-compat.sh`)
subcommands.name: plugin-compat

subcommands.about: Run the committed eval suites through the fake model and assert every case goes RED -- the falsifiability gate's real-path negative control (#619, `bash cli/scripts/eval-falsifiability.sh`). Offline, no credential
subcommands.name: eval-falsifiability

subcommands.about: Assert every `Deserialize` struct in `cli/src/api.rs` is declared in `cli/api-mirrors.json` and covers its API model's fields (#691, `bash cli/scripts/check-field-parity.sh`). Offline, no credential
subcommands.name: field-parity

subcommands.about: Assert every declared `emits` projection in `cli/api-mirrors.json` -- a `CliOutput::to_json` that hand-projects a mirror struct into a `json!` literal -- covers that struct's fields (#699, one hop downstream of `field-parity`, `bash cli/scripts/check-emit-parity.sh`). Offline, no credential
subcommands.name: emit-parity

subcommands.about: Assert the release-coupled versions agree: cli/Cargo.toml, Chart.yaml version, and appVersion (`bash scripts/check-version-consistency.sh`)
subcommands.name: version-check

subcommands.about: Assert every direct `ClassName.model_validate*(...)` call on an `_AciModel` subclass threads `READER_CONTEXT` or is a declared exception in `tools/wire-tolerance-gate/allowlist.json` (#625, following #492's forgotten-context bug, `bash scripts/check-wire-tolerance.sh`). Offline, no credential
subcommands.name: wire-tolerance

subcommands.about: Set the release version across cli/Cargo.toml + Chart.yaml version/appVersion (and refresh the CLI lockfile) so a release cut can't leave the three out of sync. Does not commit or tag

args.help: The new release version: semver `X.Y.Z` or `X.Y.Z-rc.N`
Print the planned edits without writing anything

subcommands.name: bump-version

subcommands.about: Print the machine-readable command manifest (JSON) to stdout
subcommands.long_about:
Print the machine-readable command manifest (JSON) to stdout.

Hidden, developer-facing: regenerates `cli/command-manifest.json`, which a CI drift gate keeps in lockstep with the CLI grammar. Also reachable as `dump-commands`.
subcommands.name: schema

subcommands.about: Print the committed, versioned JSON Schemas for the `--json` result outputs

args.help: The schema to print (short name like `kill`, or `kill.schema.json`). Omit to print the inventory index of all result schemas

subcommands.long_about:
Print the committed, versioned JSON Schemas for the `--json` result outputs.

With no NAME, emits the schema inventory index (`cli/schema/index.json`): every agent-facing result family, the schema file it maps to, and its version. With a NAME (e.g. `kill`, or `kill.schema.json`), emits that schema. The schemas are embedded in the binary, so this works from a released `curie` with no source checkout (issue #634).
subcommands.name: schema-index

subcommands.about: Print a self-contained primer for a coding agent driving the harness (ADR-0021)
subcommands.long_about:
Print a self-contained primer for a coding agent driving the harness (ADR-0021).

Ordered by what the agent needs first (roughly 100 lines), carrying only non-discoverable knowledge: the parity ladder, when/which decision logic, the landmines, and verify-first. Human-readable Markdown by default; The global `--json` emits a structured variant (data on stdout, human text on stderr).
subcommands.name: guide
